BP: Updated entry to the current EXFOR
format, added subentries 8-13, corrected INSITUTE:
2AUSPVI -> 2AUSIRK and contacted author.
The integrations over the solid angle were
done by fitting the angular distributions by means of
Legendre polynomials Pi so that the integrated cross
section was obtained from the first coefficient A0
after multiplying by 4pi.

x4freetext: The multiple scattering correction of the elastically
scattered neutrons is expected to be quite reliable.
Therefore, differential cross sections for the
elastic scattering were extracted. In the case of 1
0B, scattering involving the first excited state at
0.72 MeV could not be separated from the elastic
scattering. Therefore, cross sections of the highest
energy peak rather than elastic cross sections are
given.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainties given in Table IX were
obtained by adding quadratically the individual
errors disregarding any correlation.

datasets.quantExpan: Differential c/s with respect to angle

x4freetext: The target was a gas cell with an entrance foil of
5.3 mg/cm2 molybdenum and a beams top of 0.05 cm gold.
The target to sample distance was 14.0 cm, the flight
path 252.3 cm. The 10B sample was a slightly
irregularly shaped cylinder of 4 cm height, a purity
of 95.5%, and a mass of 1.80 moles.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The final error given with each data point
of the secondary neutron spectra consists of the
combined counting statistics of the individual
spectra plus contributions of various corrections:
- subtraction of white (time-uncorrelated) time-of-
flight background
- subtraction of the spectrum of the admixed isotope
and other impurities (hydrogen in 10B)
- multiple scattering correction.
For each data point these corrections are uncorrelated
and, therefore, their error contributions were added
quadratically.
These spectra were corrected for multiple
scattering using the Monte Carlo simulation performed
by the Los Alamos National Laboratory code MCNP.

x4freetext: The target was a gas cell with an entrance foil of
5.3 mg/cm2 molybdenum and a beams top of 0.05 cm gold.
The target to sample distance was 14.0 cm, the flight
path 252.3 cm. The 11B sample was a right-circular
cylinder of about 3 cm height, 1.7 cm diameter, a
purity of 90.5%, and a mass of 0.92 moles.
These spectra were corrected for multiple
scattering using the Monte Carlo simulation performed
by the Los Alamos National Laboratory code MCNP.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The final error given with each data point
of the secondary neutron spectra consists of the
combined counting statistics of the individual
spectra plus contributions of various corrections:
- subtraction of white (time-uncorrelated) time-of-
flight background
- subtraction of the spectrum of the admixed isotope
and other impurities (hydrogen in 10B)
- multiple scattering correction.
For each data point these corrections are uncorrelated
and, therefore, their error contributions were added
quadratically.

reacs.QuantHlp: Differential c/s with respect to angle

x4freetext: The angular distributions of the energy
integrated spectra. These data contain extrapolations
to zero neutron energy. To account for events below
the neutron detection bias, the following simple
procedure was believed to be good enough considering
the poor quality of the multiple scattering
correction: it was assumed that the spectrum below
the cutoff at 0.35 MeV was in the average as intense
as the spectrum between 0.35 and 2.0 MeV. By
assigning a 50% error to this extrapolation most
eventualities should be taken care of. This
integration of the double differential cross sections
over both the energy of the emitted neutrons and the
solid angle was done in two steps. First, the cross
section of the highest energy peak was converted into
the center-of-mass system and integrated over the
solid angle in this system, and then, the remaining
spectrum was integrated over the energy and over the
solid angle in the laboratory system.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainties given in Table X were
obtained by adding quadratically the individual
errors disregarding any correlation.

integrated spectra. These data contain extrapolations
to zero neutron energy. To account for events below
the neutron detection bias, the following simple
procedure was believed to be good enough considering
the poor quality of the multiple scattering
correction: it was assumed that the spectrum below
the cutoff at 0.35 MeV was in the average as intense
as the spectrum between 0.35 and 2.0 MeV. By
assigning a 50% error to this extrapolation most
eventualities should be taken care of. This
integration of the double differential cross sections
over both the energy of the emitted neutrons and the
solid angle was done in two steps. First, the cross
section of the highest energy peak was converted into
the center-of-mass system and integrated over the
solid angle in this system, and then, the remaining
spectrum was integrated over the energy and over the
solid angle in the laboratory system.
